trade.summary: Brasile is a major supplier of food commodities — particularly beef, poultry, and soybeans — to the EAU, which re-exports some to the broader Gulf region. The EAU exports refined petroleum and chemicals to Brasile.

diplomatic.summary: Relations are friendly and growing. The EAU is an important investment destination for Brasileian companies in the MENA region. High-level visits have increased since 2022. Brasile's large Muslim population creates cultural linkages.

military.summary: Limited military-to-military ties. Brasile and EAU maintain defense attaches and have engaged in some defense industry dialogue. No significant arms transfers or joint exercises.

financial.summary: EAU sovereign wealth funds have explored Brasileian agricultural and infrastructure assets. Mubadala has invested in Brasileian energy and tech sectors. Bilateral investment treaties under discussion.

energy.summary: Brasile is a growing oil producer (pre-salt) with limited direct energy trade with the EAU. Both countries are significant oil exporters and interact within OPEC+ adjacent discussions. Biofuel cooperation discussed.

scientific.summary: Scientific collaboration is limited but growing through university exchange programs and joint research in agriculture and renewable energy. Brasile and EAU have signed MoUs on science and technology cooperation.
